heroHeadline: Mexican Navy Captures Los Mayos Leader 'El Patas' in Culiacán; El Mayo Sentencing Approaches
heroSubtitle: 614 days of intra-cartel war following Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada's alleged kidnapping — 2,113+ homicides, 9,000+ families displaced. La Mayiza controls approximately 90% of former Chapitos territory as Mexican Navy operations intensify: Los Mayos faction leader Omar Torres 'El Patas' was captured in Culiacán on March 19, and El Mayo Zambada faces a life sentence with his April 13 sentencing hearing approaching. Chapitos cooperation with US prosecutors continues to generate new indictments.
footerNote: Data sourced from Noroeste, Riodoce, Proceso, InSight Crime, Reuters, AP, Animal Político, ACLED, DEA, SEDENA, SEMAR, Al Jazeera, AFP
